growth_records.strain: CAB2021 (pSOL1-integrated, sadh + hydG from C. beijerinckii NRRL B593; IBE producer)

up.method: calculated from biofuel volumetric productivity / biofuel-on-glucose mass yield = 1 / 0.31 = 3.23 g glucose/L/h; specific qS not derivable (steady-state biomass gDW/L not given numerically in text)

growth_records.cite: Ehsaan et al. Chromosomal integration of the pSOL1 megaplasmid of Clostridium acetobutylicum for continuous and stable advanced biofuels production. Nature Microbiology (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-024-01714-w. [PMC11222136]
growth_records.snippet: The best compromise between residual glucose, titre and productivity was observed at a dilution rate of 0.075 h-1 with a biofuel titre of 13.4 g l-1, a productivity of 1 g l-1 h-1 and a yield of 0.31 g g-1.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu = D = 0.075 h^-1. Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.075 = 9.24 h (calculated). Reported: biofuel(IBE) titre 13.4 g/L, volumetric productivity 1 g/L/h, mass yield 0.31 g biofuel/g glucose. Volumetric glucose consumption = productivity/yield = 1/0.31 = 3.23 g/L/h (calculated). Specific qS/qP (mmol/gDW/h) not derivable because steady-state DCW (gDW/L) is only shown graphically (Fig 2b), not stated numerically. Medium GMM per litre as defined in Methods (bioreactor used base GMM = 50 g/L glucose; the 60 g/L-glucose / ammonium-acetate modification applied only to serum-bottle batch cultures). pH 4.4, 35 C stated.

growth_records.strain: CAB2021 (pSOL1-integrated, sadh + hydG from C. beijerinckii NRRL B593; IBE producer)

up.method: calculated from biofuel volumetric productivity / yield = 1.3 / 0.3 = 4.33 g glucose consumed/L/h; note high residual glucose (glucose not fully consumed) at this dilution rate

growth_records.cite: Ehsaan et al. Chromosomal integration of the pSOL1 megaplasmid of Clostridium acetobutylicum for continuous and stable advanced biofuels production. Nature Microbiology (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-024-01714-w. [PMC11222136]
growth_records.snippet: it was possible to produce biofuels at high yield (0.3 g g-1) and productivity (1.3 g l-1 h-1) for the highest dilution rate evaluated, although under this condition high residual glucose concentrations were observed.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu = D. Highest dilution rate evaluated = 0.75 h^-1 (Methods: multi-stage chemostat stage 4 raised D to 0.75 h^-1; Fig 2b shows four dilution rates = 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.75). mu = 0.75 h^-1, doubling = ln2/0.75 = 0.924 h (calculated). Reported yield 0.3 g biofuel/g glucose, volumetric productivity 1.3 g/L/h. Volumetric glucose consumption = 1.3/0.3 = 4.33 g/L/h (calculated); high residual glucose noted (incomplete uptake). Confidence medium because the numeric coupling of 'highest dilution rate' to exactly 0.75 h^-1 is inferred from Methods stage list; specific qS not derivable (no numeric DCW).

growth_records.strain: CAB2021 (pSOL1-integrated, sadh + hydG from C. beijerinckii NRRL B593; IBE producer)

up.method: calculated from biofuel volumetric productivity / yield = 15.5 / 0.31 = 50 g glucose/L/h; specific qS not derivable (numeric DCW/OD only in figure)

growth_records.cite: Ehsaan et al. Chromosomal integration of the pSOL1 megaplasmid of Clostridium acetobutylicum for continuous and stable advanced biofuels production. Nature Microbiology (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-024-01714-w. [PMC11222136]
growth_records.snippet: In GMM, the performances obtained in terms of yield (0.31 g g-1), titre (15.4 g l-1) and productivities (15.5 g l-1 h-1) were the highest ever reported for the continuous production of an advanced IBE fuel mixture ... a cell bleeding rate w
growth_records.notes: Membrane cell-recycle (perfusion) continuous culture with total cell retention and cell bleed at 0.05 h^-1; at steady state the specific growth rate equals the cell bleed rate, so mu ~= 0.05 h^-1 (NOT the permeate dilution rate, which is decoupled from growth because biomass is retained). Doubling = ln2/0.05 = 13.86 h (calculated). Reported: yield 0.31 g biofuel/g glucose, titre 15.4 g/L, volumetric productivity 15.5 g/L/h. Volumetric glucose consumption = 15.5/0.31 = 50 g/L/h (calculated). Specific qS/qP not derivable (biomass reported as DCW only graphically, OD600 reached 200). Confidence medium: mu=cell-bleed-rate is the standard perfusion steady-state relation but not stated verbatim.

up.method: consumed carbon source (CRM contains 50 g/L glucose); numeric uptake not stated in text

sec.method: reported qualitatively (Fig 1b left axis); numeric values only in figure/source data
reported qualitatively (Fig 1b left axis); numeric values only in figure/source data

growth_records.cite: Ehsaan et al. Chromosomal integration of the pSOL1 megaplasmid of Clostridium acetobutylicum for continuous and stable advanced biofuels production. Nature Microbiology (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-024-01714-w. [PMC11222136]
growth_records.snippet: Chemostat cultures, in CRM, at 35 C, a dilution rate of 0.05 h-1 and a pH of 4.8, were used to evaluate the strains' degeneration ... the former [wild-type] degenerated within 30 days, losing the ability to produce solvents concomitant with
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu = D = 0.05 h^-1; doubling = ln2/0.05 = 13.86 h (calculated). Distinct growth condition (wild-type ATCC 824, CRM, degeneration test). Products butanol and butyrate reported (Fig 1b) but numeric titres/fluxes are only in the figure/source-data xlsx, not in text, so rates left null. Glucose is the carbon source (CRM 50 g/L) but uptake not quantified in text. Medium CRM per litre as defined in Methods.

growth_records.medium: In silico nutrient-rich minimal medium, fructose sole carbon source

up.method: imposed FBA uptake bound (sole carbon source)

growth_records.cite: Foster et al. Modeling Growth Kinetics, Interspecies Cell Fusion, and Metabolism of a Clostridium acetobutylicum/Clostridium ljungdahlii Syntrophic Coculture. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.01325-20. [PMC8573953]
growth_records.snippet: Active metabolic network and maximum growth rate using 5 mmol gDW−1 h−1 fructose as the sole carbon source ... Cac nonhybrid (iCAC802) ... Max growth rate (h−1) 0.79
growth_records.notes: Model (FBA) MAXIMUM growth rate, not an experimental measurement: iCAC802 gave mu=0.79 h-1 with imposed fructose uptake of 5 mmol/gDW/h (sole carbon, nutrient-rich in silico). doubling_time = ln2/0.79 = 0.877 h (calculated). Hybrid (cell-fusion) variant gave 0.81 h-1 (2.5% higher) but is a hypothetical fused construct, not recorded. Flagged low confidence because it is a theoretical GEM output, not measured physiology.

growth_records.medium: Turbo Clostridium Growth Medium (CGM) + glucose + fructose

up.method: Cac substrate; consumption rate not numerically reported in text
Cac and Clj substrate; rate not numerically reported in text

sec.method: experimentally measured extracellular titer at end of culture

sec.compound: products (acetate, butyrate, butanol, acetone, ethanol, isopropanol, 2,3-butanediol, acetoin, CO2, H2)
sec.method: observed fermentation products; per-product experimental titers reported only in figures, not numerically in text (butanol/butyrate model deviated at most 2.0/0.81 g/L from experiment)

growth_records.cite: Foster et al. Modeling Growth Kinetics, Interspecies Cell Fusion, and Metabolism of a Clostridium acetobutylicum/Clostridium ljungdahlii Syntrophic Coculture. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.01325-20. [PMC8573953]
growth_records.snippet: Batch culture initial loading conditions were as follows: 5 g/liter fructose and 80 g/liter glucose supplemented with Turbo Clostridium growth medium (CGM)... Optical density measurements at 600 nm (OD600) were used to estimate specific gro
growth_records.notes: Real experimental syntrophic batch coculture with fully specified medium. Species-specific experimental growth rates were estimated from interpolated OD600 curves and used as growth-model parameters, but the numeric mu values for Cac and Clj are not stated in the text (embedded in figures/model). Only explicit experimental product value in text is L-lactate final titer 0.03 g/L. Cac ferments glucose+fructose to acetate/butyrate/butanol/acetone/ethanol/CO2/H2; Clj (cannot use glucose) grows on Cac-evolved CO2/H2 producing acetate+ethanol. Record retained for its complete medium composition; no numeric rate/flux reported experimentally in text.

growth_records.medium: Clostridium growth medium (CGM) + 0.5% xylose; no supplemental sugar [(+)None]

up.method: HPLC (Aminex HPX-87H) sugar depletion normalized to average OD600 (Fig. 2C)

growth_records.cite: Servinsky MD et al. Arabinose-Induced Catabolite Repression as a Mechanism for Pentose Hierarchy Control in Clostridium acetobutylicum ATCC 824. mSystems (2018). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00064-18. [PMC6199471]
growth_records.method: reported (growth/doubling shown as growth curve in Fig. 2A; no numeric mu or td value in text)
growth_records.snippet: Xylose in (+)None and (+)Xyl was depleted initially at a rate of between 3.0 and 4.0 delta-mM/OD600(avg)/h during the first 2 h postaddition and stayed above 2.0 ... The final (+)None and (+)Xyl acetate-to-butyrate ratios were similar, 0.51
growth_records.notes: Baseline condition (xylose-only, water control). Xylose uptake reported as delta-xylose(mM)/OD600(avg)/h, NOT mmol/gDW/h; a gDW conversion is not derivable because no gDW-per-OD600 factor is given in the paper. No numeric growth rate: doubling times are only shown graphically (Fig. 2A). pH of CGM not stated. Anaerobic obligate anaerobe (Coy chamber 5% H2/5% CO2/90% N2), 37 C, static. Products acetate/butyrate/acetone/butanol/ethanol measured by HPLC but only acetate:butyrate ratio (0.51 +/- 0.06) given numerically for this condition.

growth_records.medium: CGM + 0.5% xylose, supplemented at OD600 0.4 with 0.25% xylose [(+)Xyl]

up.method: HPLC sugar depletion normalized to average OD600 (Fig. 2B,C)

growth_records.cite: Servinsky MD et al. Arabinose-Induced Catabolite Repression as a Mechanism for Pentose Hierarchy Control in Clostridium acetobutylicum ATCC 824. mSystems (2018). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00064-18. [PMC6199471]
growth_records.method: reported (Fig. 2A; similar doubling time to (+)None, exponential growth extended ~2 h beyond (+)None; no numeric value)
growth_records.snippet: Xylose in (+)None and (+)Xyl was depleted initially at a rate of between 3.0 and 4.0 delta-mM/OD600(avg)/h ... The final (+)None and (+)Xyl acetate-to-butyrate ratios were similar, 0.51 +/- 0.06 and 0.48 +/- 0.03, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Xylose-supplemented condition. Xylose uptake same 3.0-4.0 then >2.0 delta-mM/OD600/h as (+)None; adding 0.25% xylose extended exponential growth ~2 h. Rate units are per-OD600, not mmol/gDW/h (no gDW/OD conversion in paper). Acetate:butyrate ratio 0.48 +/- 0.03. No numeric mu/td. Anaerobic, 37 C, static.

growth_records.medium: CGM + 0.5% xylose, supplemented at OD600 0.4 with 0.25% glucose [(+)Glu]

up.method: HPLC sugar depletion normalized to average OD600 (Fig. 2B,C)

growth_records.cite: Servinsky MD et al. Arabinose-Induced Catabolite Repression as a Mechanism for Pentose Hierarchy Control in Clostridium acetobutylicum ATCC 824. mSystems (2018). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00064-18. [PMC6199471]
growth_records.method: reported (Fig. 2A; growth comparable to control for first 2 h, increased growth rate/reached max doubling time between hours 3 and 5; no numeric value)
growth_records.snippet: Xylose consumption in (+)Glu was dramatically slowed, dropping to below 1.0 delta-mM/OD600(avg)/h by hour 3 and reaching 0.36 delta-mM/OD600(avg)/h at hour 5, at which point the glucose was exhausted ... final ratio of 0.51 +/- 0.02.
growth_records.notes: Glucose-supplement condition demonstrating catabolite repression of xylose. Xylose uptake fell to 0.36 delta-mM/OD600/h at hr5; glucose depleted ~hr5; >25% xylose left after 10 h. Rate units per-OD600, not mmol/gDW/h. Acetate:butyrate 0.51 +/- 0.02. No numeric mu/td (Fig. 2A only). Anaerobic, 37 C, static.

growth_records.medium: CGM + 0.5% xylose, supplemented at OD600 0.4 with 0.25% arabinose [(+)Ara]

up.method: HPLC sugar depletion normalized to average OD600 (Fig. 2B,C)

growth_records.cite: Servinsky MD et al. Arabinose-Induced Catabolite Repression as a Mechanism for Pentose Hierarchy Control in Clostridium acetobutylicum ATCC 824. mSystems (2018). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00064-18. [PMC6199471]
growth_records.method: reported (Fig. 2A; growth rate significantly increased over other conditions, reached maximum doubling time by 2nd hour after supplementation; no numeric value, Student's t test P<0.01 vs other conditions)
growth_records.snippet: Xylose consumption in (+)Ara was even lower than was observed in (+)Glu after 2 h [1.24 versus 1.72 delta-mM/OD600(avg)/h] and equivalent to it after 3 h [0.87 ...]. ... (+)Ara had much higher concentrations of acetate, 39 +/- 0.4 mM ... fi
growth_records.notes: Arabinose-supplement condition: arabinose preferred over xylose. Xylose uptake 1.24 (hr2) and 0.87 (hr3) delta-mM/OD600/h; arabinose depleted ~4 h, xylose ~8 h. Rate units per-OD600, not mmol/gDW/h. Acetate final titer 39 +/- 0.4 mM (highest), acetate:butyrate 1.3 +/- 0.2 indicating shift from pentose-phosphate to phosphoketolase pathway. Growth rate significantly increased (P<0.01) but only shown graphically (Fig. 2A); no numeric mu/td. Anaerobic, 37 C, static.

growth_records.medium: not stated (review; primary source Liu et al., 2014)

sec.method: batch endpoint titer, review Table 2 (cited from Liu et al., 2014)

growth_records.cite: Zhang, Huifang et al. Clostridium acetobutylicum Biofilm: Advances in Understanding the Basis. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2021.658568. [PMC8209462]
growth_records.snippet: C. acetobutylicum B3 | Free cells | Butanol titer 11.8 g/L | Solvent yield 0.34 g/g | Solvent productivity 0.25 g/L/h | Liu et al., 2014 (Table 2).
growth_records.notes: REVIEW-reproduced value (Table 2), NOT primary data of this paper. Original citation: Liu et al., 2014. Free (planktonic) cell batch reference. Volumetric productivity (g/L/h) and solvent yield (g/g) only; no growth rate, biomass, or per-gDW uptake/secretion rates. Anaerobic ABE fermentation assumed from Clostridium biology.

growth_records.medium: not stated (review; primary source Huang et al., 2004)

sec.method: best butanol productivity at D=0.9 h-1, review text + Table 2 (cited from Huang et al., 2004)

growth_records.cite: Zhang, Huifang et al. Clostridium acetobutylicum Biofilm: Advances in Understanding the Basis. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2021.658568. [PMC8209462]
growth_records.snippet: Huang et al. (2004) used C. acetobutylicum biofilm ... continuously performed for more than 600 h, and the best butanol productivity of 4.6 g/L/h was obtained at a dilution rate of 0.9 h-1. Table 2: ATCC 55025, ~5 g/L, 0.42 g/g, 4.6 g/L/h.
growth_records.notes: REVIEW-reproduced (Table 2 + text), original: Huang et al., 2004. Biofilm-immobilized continuous fermentation; dilution rate 0.9 h-1 is the REACTOR dilution rate, NOT growth rate (cells immobilized/retained), so mu not inferred. Volumetric productivity 4.6 g/L/h butanol, solvent yield 0.42 g/g. No per-gDW or growth-rate data.

growth_records.medium: not stated (review; primary source Liu et al., 2013)

up.method: text states glucose was completely consumed in the repeated-batch process (no rate given)

sec.method: repeated-batch titer, review Table 2 + text (cited); 21% higher than planktonic cells
volumetric productivity, review Table 2 + text (cited from Liu et al., 2013)

growth_records.cite: Zhang, Huifang et al. Clostridium acetobutylicum Biofilm: Advances in Understanding the Basis. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2021.658568. [PMC8209462]
growth_records.snippet: Liu et al. (2013) developed a repeated batch fermentation ... shortened from 72 to 12-14 h per batch ... glucose could be completely consumed ... a high butanol titer of 15.6 g/L was achieved with a solvent productivity of 1.88 g/L/h. Table
growth_records.notes: REVIEW-reproduced (Table 2 + text), original: Liu et al., 2013. C. acetobutylicum B3 biofilm repeated-batch. Butanol titer 15.6 g/L (21% > planktonic), solvent yield 0.38 g/g, productivity 1.88 g/L/h; batch time 12-14 h (vs 72 h traditional); glucose fully consumed. No quantitative uptake rate or growth rate given.
